ml_D_D.filesystem package

Submodules

ml_D_D.filesystem.save module

filesystem/save.py Save and load ML Forge project files (.mlf).

ml_D_D.filesystem.save.load_project(path: str) → None[source]

Load an ML Forge project from a file and restore its full state.

This function: - Reads and parses the project file (JSON format). - Validates file version compatibility. - Clears existing tabs and UI state. - Reconstructs all tabs, nodes, and links. - Restores UI elements such as active tab and pipeline state.

It also triggers post-load processes like undo menu refresh, pipeline updates, and optional dataset-based inference.

Parameters:

path (str) – The file path of the project to load.

Returns:

None

ml_D_D.filesystem.save.open_load_dialog() → None[source]

Open a file dialog for loading a project.

Allows the user to select a project file and triggers loading of the selected file into the application.

Returns:

None

ml_D_D.filesystem.save.open_save_dialog() → None[source]

Open a file dialog for saving the current project.

Allows the user to select a file path and ensures the correct file extension is applied. Triggers project saving upon confirmation.

Returns:

None

ml_D_D.filesystem.save.save_current() → None[source]

Save the project to the currently associated file path.

If a file path is already known, the project is saved directly. Otherwise, a save dialog is opened for the user to choose a path.

Returns:

None

ml_D_D.filesystem.save.save_project(path: str) → None[source]

Save the current ML Forge project to a file.

This function serializes all tabs, nodes, and links into a structured JSON format and writes it to disk. It also updates the current file reference and refreshes the UI status.

Parameters:

path (str) – The file path where the project should be saved.

Returns:

None
